methods.description: This method calculates the wetland methane emissions for each grid cell and time step using the ORCHIDEE-WET model. It incorporates the extent and flux density of wetlands with varying water table depths, using a combination of the TOPMODEL approach and a modified version of the Walter et al. (2001) model.

steps.description: Compute wetland CH4 emissions for each grid cell and time step using the ORCHIDEE-WET model.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate wetland methane emissions using ORCHIDEE-WET model

steps.description: Compute the extent and CH4 flux density of wetlands with a water table depth equal to WTD i using a coupling of ORCHIDEE-WET with TOPMODEL and a modified Walter et al. (2001) model.
steps.searchable_summary: compute wetland extent and flux density using TOPMODEL and modified Walter model

steps.description: Simulate soil water content resulting from a hydrologic budget accounting for inputs and losses.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate soil water content from hydrologic budget

steps.description: Distribute mean soil water deficit over each grid cell as a function of the subgrid topographic index distribution.
steps.searchable_summary: distribute soil water deficit using topographic index

steps.description: Compute inundated wetland extents from field capacity extents.
steps.searchable_summary: compute inundated wetland extents from field capacity

steps.description: Parameterize topographic index distribution shift to simulate current global wetland fraction.
steps.searchable_summary: parameterize topographic index shift for wetland fraction simulation

steps.description: Compute CH4 flux densities using a modified Walter et al. (2001) model with labile carbon pool from ORCHIDEE-WET.
steps.searchable_summary: compute CH4 flux densities using modified Walter model and labile carbon pool

steps.description: Optimize parameter α0 against three sites and extrapolate at large band scale.
steps.searchable_summary: optimize and extrapolate parameter α0 for methanogenesis
